question.text: An investment of Rs. 50,000 grows to Rs. 85,000 over 5 years. The Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) is closest to:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CAGR = (End Value / Beginning Value)^(1/n) - 1 = (85,000/50,000)^(1/5) - 1 = (1.7)^(0.2) - 1 = 1.1120 - 1 = 11.20%. **The Trap:** Simple return would be (85,000 - 50,000)/50,000 = 70% over 5 years = 14% per year. But CAGR accounts for compounding and is lower. **Cross-Exam Context:** CAGR is the standard return measure in mutual funds (X-A, V-A, V-B) and portfolio evaluation (XXI-B). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always use CAGR for multi-year returns — simple annualized returns overstate performance.
